[How to upgrade to pro+ plan? - Support / Help - Cursor - Community Forum](https://forum.cursor.com/t/how-to-upgrade-to-pro-plan/109001)\n\n# Cursor IDE Subscription Upgrade: $20 to $60 Proration Billing\n\n## Upgrading from Pro to Pro+\n\nTo upgrade from Pro ($20/month) to Pro+ ($60/month), use this direct link: &#96;https://www.cursor.com/api/auth/checkoutDeepControl?tier=pro_plus&#96;[1] Pro+ is not displayed on the main pricing page but offers 3x the rate limits for agents.[5]\n\n## Prorated Billing for Upgrades\n\n**How proration works:**[1]\n- Your new Pro+ monthly billing starts immediately\n- You receive a prorated refund for any unused time on your current plan\n- Your previous subscription cancels automatically\n- Refunds typically process in 3-5 business days\n\n**Example:** If you paid $120 annually and upgrade after 3 months, you'd be charged the Pro+ rate immediately while receiving a refund for the 9 unused months ($90).[1]\n\n## Important Note\n\nThere may be proration issues with the direct checkout link. One Cursor engineer noted the link \"may not correctly pro-rate at this time\" and recommended canceling your current subscription and waiting for your billing period to end before upgrading to ensure proper billing.[5]\n\n## General Billing Rules\n\nFor Business accounts with multiple seats, Cursor uses prorated billing when team size changes, charging only for the actual time each seat is used.[2] You can switch between monthly and annual billing, though switching from yearly to monthly requires contacting support.[2][3]",

The business option is the Teams plan at $40/user/month, which includes shared chats, centralized billing, usage analytics, SSO, and role-based access control.[4][5]\n\n**Upgrade Process:**\nTo upgrade, go to cursor.com/dashboard and click the upgrade button for the Teams plan you want.[4] However, users have reported technical issues during the upgrade process, with some encountering checkout errors when attempting to upgrade from Pro to Teams.[1]\n\n**Billing Considerations:**\n- When switching plans, you manage your subscription through the dashboard's Billing and Invoices section.[3]\n- If you're on annual Pro billing, you cannot automatically switch to another plan mid-year through self-serve.
